steps.title: Identify what would weaken the claim
steps.content: The student claims that emotionality of movie reviews is unrelated to box office success. To weaken this, we need a finding showing that emotionality does predict box office earnings — a direct link between the two variables.

steps.title: Test each choice against that standard
steps.content:
• Choice A — Movies that had the highest average emotionality scores received the lowest average star ratings on the movie rating website.: connects emotionality to star ratings, not to box office earnings — irrelevant to the student's claim. ✗
  • Choice B — The average emotionality score of a movie's reviews was a positive predictor of that movie's box office earnings.: directly links emotionality to box office success, contradicting the student's claim that the two are unrelated. ✓
  • Choice C — More than half of the movies that the team examined received an average star rating of 3 out of 5 stars.: describes star ratings only; says nothing about emotionality or its relationship to earnings. ✗
  • Choice D — The movies that were most successful at the box office tended to have high average star ratings.: links star ratings to box office success, but does not address emotionality at all. ✗
That matches Choice B — The average emotionality score of a movie's reviews was a positive predictor of that movie's box office earnings..
